target.title: fix(gateway): clear provider auth prewarm on stop
target.body:
## Summary

- Clear provider-auth prewarm state from the full-gateway prewarm sidecar's `stop()` path.
- Cancel the active prewarm through the existing provider-auth state owner instead of relying on process shutdown.
- Preserve the actual local-agent issue as follow-up by removing the unsupported CLI-wide cleanup and closing claim.
- Add focused gateway and provider-auth lifecycle coverage, and keep CLI exit coverage aligned with the CLI resources it actually owns.

Related to #92464

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Full-gateway provider-auth prewarm can start an off-main-thread provider-auth warm worker. When the gateway is stopped before or during that prewarm, the gateway-owned sidecar should clear provider-auth state so the tracked warm worker is cancelled by its lifecycle owner instead of being left to process shutdown.

**Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `6c4e6e93b6b88849aa268095dd43fc8356330665`. The real foreground gateway command was run against a temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` with 450 local agents to keep the provider-auth worker active long enough to stop during prewarm. No provider credentials, network model calls, or external model calls were used. A `NODE_OPTIONS --import=/tmp/.../provider-auth-worker-observer.mjs` hook only recorded `node:worker_threads` provider-auth worker start/terminate events; it did not change OpenClaw source files or credentials.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node /tmp/openclaw-92493-gateway-proof.mjs
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/cli/run-main.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts
git diff --check
```

**Evidence after fix:** A real foreground gateway reached ready, the provider-auth worker started and was still active when `SIGTERM` was sent, `clearCurrentProviderAuthState()` caused the tracked worker's `terminate()` path to run, and the gateway exited cleanly with no surviving gateway process.

```json
{
  "proofRoot": "/tmp/openclaw-92493-gateway-proof-H0awnb",
  "command": "node openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 33975 --bind loopback",
  "head": "6c4e6e93b6b88849aa268095dd43fc8356330665",
  "startedAt": "2026-06-13T01:50:30.173Z",
  "readyAt": "2026-06-13T01:50:36.716Z",
  "workerStartAt": "2026-06-13T01:51:45.470Z",
  "afterExitAt": "2026-06-13T01:51:47.098Z",
  "gatewayPid": 1532730,
  "agentsConfigured": 450,
  "workerHadExitedBeforeSignal": false,
  "threadsAtReady": 11,
  "threadsWithWorkerActive": 12,
  "exitResult": { "code": 0, "signal": null },
  "gatewayProcessAliveAfterExit": false,
  "providerAuthWorkerEvents": [
    {
      "event": "provider-auth-worker-start",
      "threadId": 1,
      "filename": "file:///home/0668001029/openclaw-92464/dist/agents/model-provider-auth.worker.js"
    },
    { "event": "provider-auth-worker-online", "threadId": 1 },
    { "event": "provider-auth-worker-terminate-called", "threadId": 1 }
  ],
  "psWhileActive": [
    "1532730 1532719 Rl   openclaw"
  ],
  "psAfterStop": [
    "no openclaw gateway child process remained"
  ]
}
```

The gateway log for the same run shows the real sidecars reached ready, the process received `SIGTERM`, and shutdown completed cleanly:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=8.0 postReadySidecarCount=2.0
[gateway] startup trace: post-ready.agent-runtime-plugins 192.1ms total=71179.2ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[gateway] signal SIGTERM received
[gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
[shutdown] started: gateway stopping
[shutdown] completed cleanly in 314ms
```

Focused lifecycle tests also cover both stop timings: before the delayed prewarm starts and while a prewarm is already active. The provider-auth worker test uses a real worker-thread script that would write a marker after its delay; after `clearCurrentProviderAuthState()` runs, the warm promise settles and the marker is absent, proving the active worker was terminated.

```text
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  102 passed (102)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  91 passed (91)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  19 passed (19)

[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 59.12s
```

Additional static checks:

```text
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/cli/run-main.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts
# no output

git diff --check
# no output
```

**Observed result after fix:** Stopping a real foreground gateway while provider-auth prewarm was active called the tracked provider-auth worker's terminate path, the gateway shutdown finished cleanly with exit code 0, and the gateway PID was gone after stop. Automated coverage confirms the sidecar also clears provider-auth state when stopped before the delayed prewarm fires.

**What was not tested:** This PR no longer claims to fix the scale-sensitive `openclaw agent --local --json` retained-resource report from #92464. The exact Docker image, 10,000-entry local memory workload, external OpenAI provider credentials, and before/after active-handle comparison for the local-agent hang were not tested here. Those need a separate root-cause fix for the local-agent memory/embedding lifecycle before the issue can be closed.
